FAQ and troubleshooting

Common fixes for npm installs, missing generated sites, Beads coordination, and npm Trusted Publishing releases.

Home / Articles
ArticleFor users faqtroubleshootingrelease

Summary

Common fixes for npm installs, missing generated sites, Beads coordination, and npm Trusted Publishing releases.

Article

If npm installs Wikiwiki in the wrong place, check npm prefix. In non-JavaScript repos, create a local package.json first or use npm install --prefix "$PWD" --save-dev @thjodann/wk so npm does not climb to an ancestor project.

If the generated site is missing, run wk site --audience all or the repo's wiki:site script when present. A bare /wk invocation should show an existing generated site, but it should not start a server or generate a site on its own.

If a repo uses Beads, Wikiwiki detects .beads/ and can include read-only task context when explicitly enabled. Beads remains the task system; Wikiwiki records only durable project knowledge and public wiki content.

If npm Trusted Publishing rejects a release, publish manually with the package owner account and npm 2FA, then let the GitHub Release workflow rerun or skip the already-published version. npm versions are immutable, so every release needs a fresh package version.
